urgency.message: 4 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: The FCC Wants to Kill Burner Phones
items.summary: Plus: AI bug hunting fuels Microsoft’s biggest-ever Patch Tuesday, ShinyHunters ransomware gang exploits an Oracle zero-day, and more.

items.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
items.summary: The US government has ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, forcing the company to suspend both models worldwide. Anthropic is complying but disputes the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability widely available elsewhere. [...]

items.title: Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
items.summary:
Anthropic takes Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent use by foreign nationals.
The post Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
items.summary: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national securi

items.title: Tracing Digital Intent: New MacOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Discovered
items.summary:
Unit 42 has discovered a new macOS Tahoe 26 forensic artifact that tracks user menu selections across the operating system. Learn more here. 
The post Tracing Digital Intent: New MacOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Discovered appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
items.summary: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data.

items.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
items.summary: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
items.summary:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.

The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF r

items.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
items.summary:
Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans.

The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (P

items.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
items.summary: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]

items.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
items.summary:
Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself.

Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in,

items.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
items.summary: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]

items.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
items.summary: About 7 million customers of the genetics testing company had their data stolen by hackers starting in April 2023, and many had their information posted on the dark web.

items.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock
items.summary: It is the first lapse of the spy program, known as Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), since it was passed into law in 2008.

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
items.summary: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]

items.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
items.summary:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ICS device exposure remains flat as attack surface widens, Microsoft issues incident response playbook for AI, IBM and AT&T accused of hack cover-ups.
The post In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million

items.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
items.summary: The penalty is the largest ever issued by the commission for a personal data breach, surpassing the record 134.8 billion won ($88.8 million) fine levied against SK Telecom earlier this year.

items.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
items.summary: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]

items.title: Ransomware Payment Crypto Laundering Platform Taken Out by FBI and Europol
items.summary: Domain of dark web money laundering platform AudiA6 seized and suspects arrested in joint operation by the FBI, Europol and others

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
items.summary: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 24
items.summary: Authorities seize crypto-laundering network and fake recruitment sites, JDY botnet targets U.S. military, and Miasma worm infects Microsoft and PyPi repos.

items.title: GitHub to Update npm to Thwart Software Supply Chain Attacks
items.summary: NPM, part of GitHub, announced a new version of the npm package manager with several security improvements, including disabling install scripts

items.title: Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday
items.summary:
Industry professionals comment on various aspects of Fable 5, including dual-use capabilities, safeguards, and tiered access.
The post Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9970, (Fri, Jun 12th)
Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines.

Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted usi

items.title: Microsoft fixes Windows update failures linked to WUSA installer
items.summary: Microsoft has fixed a known issue that caused Windows updates released since May 2025 to fail when installed via the Windows Update Standalone Installer (WUSA) from a network share. [...]

items.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
items.summary:
The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform.
The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
items.summary:
For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now.

The threat landscape has

items.title: Over 80% of Sports Organizations Targeted by Hackers in the Last Year
items.summary: As the FIFA World Cup 2026 kicks off, a new Darktrace report warns that sports teams and bodies are a major target for cyber criminals

items.title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
items.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]

items.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution.

LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artif

items.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
items.summary:
The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges.
The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
The browser refresh resolved critical and high-severity security defects, including a dozen use-after-free bugs.
The post Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SentinelOne + Claude: Integrations for AI Visibility, Governance, and Defense
items.summary: Learn how SentinelOne empowers modern enterprises to safely adopt Claude with Prompt Security, AI SIEM, and Wayfinder Frontier AI.

items.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
items.summary:
An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday.

The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took place between October 2025 and February 2026, and saw authorities from 13 countries in the Middle Ea

items.title: Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak
items.summary:
An AI hacker claims to have achieved a prompt-based jailbreak shortly after Fable 5’s launch, but Anthropic says it’s not a real jailbreak.
The post Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
items.summary:
Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation.
The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.summary: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: CISA BOD 26-04: Frequently asked questions about the new risk-based patching directive
items.summary: CISA issued BOD 26-04, which replaces BOD 22-01 with a four-variable vulnerability prioritization model requiring federal agencies to patch the most dangerous vulnerabilities in as few as three days.Key takeawaysBOD 26-04 replaces BOD 22-01 with a four-variable risk model that assigns graduated reme

items.title: Grok Is Still Hosting Sexualized Deepfakes of Famous Women
items.summary: A WIRED investigation found dozens of “nudified” deepfake images and videos on Grok's website, including nonconsensual depictions of celebrities and at least one prominent US politician.

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.summary: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: A tale of two eras
items.summary: In this week’s newsletter, Amy reminisces on the tech toys of their childhood, inspired by a hilarious lesson about why your digital privacy shouldn't be left on an open channel.
